rulings.Case Title: DAFNE ENGSTROM ET AL VS. EUGENE WILKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 21, 2018 line 7. Defendant Jpmorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'S Demurrer To Complaint Hearing Required. Unless the questions bellow are adequately addressed, the Court is inclined to sustain the demurrer without leave to amend. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on Dec. 21, 2018 Plaintiff shall file and serve responses to the following: (1) CC 3399 applies "when ¿ a written contract does not truly express the intention of the parties;" identify all the parties to the contract within the meaning of CC 3399 by name; (2) identify the basis for reformation (a) fraud; and/or (b) mutual mistake of parties; and/or (c) mistake of one party; (3) if the basis is (a), provide citation to cases where reformation was based on fraud perpetrated by a non-party to the contract; (4) if the basis is (b), identify facts that may be alleged to support mistake by each party to the contract identified in response to question 1; (5) if the basis is (c), (i) if Plaintiff claims that WaMu is a party to the contract (within the meaning of CC 3399) identify facts that may be alleged to support a claim that WaMu knew or suspected that Plaintiff was mistaken at the time of the contract formation and cite authorities that reformation claim may be brought against non-original party to the contract; and state whether Chase is a "third person" within the meaning of CC 3399 (without argument as to whether Chase acquired rights in good faith and for value) (ii) if Plaintiff claims that Chase is a party to the contract (within the meaning of CC 3399) identify facts that may be alleged to support a claim that Chase knew of suspected that Plaintiff was mistaken at the time of contract formation; if Plaintiff claims that basis for reformation is Chase's knowledge or suspicion of Wilkin's fraud in 2008, i.e. a year after the contract was formed, P shall cite authority and facts supporting such assertion. The Court does not require and does not authorize any further argument. In Plaintiff's responsive brief Plaintiff shall include the questions. The responses to the questions shall identify parties by name; facts (not conclusions or arguments) in a concise manner; and authorities with pin cites. If no facts or authorities may be stated in good faith, Plaintiff shall so state in response to the respective question. Plaintiff shall also provide copies of the cases cited to the Court and to the opposing side with relevant portions of the cases highlighted. Additionally, Plaintiff's Counsel shall be prepared to address why the Court should not set a hearing on Order to Show Cause Re: Failure to Comply with CCP 430.41. = (501/CFH)
